title: 10 best herbal creams for oily skin

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema.
Antifungal properties: Tea tree oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause skin issues like dandruff, psoriasis, and ringworm, which can exacerbate oily skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with oily skin because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, helps to reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation, thereby preventing clogged pores and excessive oil production. Additionally, tea tree oil has astringent properties that help to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing oiliness and leaving the skin feeling clean and refreshed. As a result, it can help to regulate oil production and improve overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps reduce oil production and inflammation, thereby controlling sebum levels and minimizing acne.
Limonene: It has astringent and antiseptic properties that help balance the skin's pH, reduce oiliness, and minimize the appearance of pores.
Caryophyllene oxide: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which help soothe and calm oily skin, reducing redness and preventing infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, are commonly used in creams for oily skin because of their antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help control oil production and reduce acne.

Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make creams for oily skin due to their antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
Seeds: The seeds of Melaleuca alternifolia are sometimes used to make creams for oily skin because of their astringent properties that help to dry up excess oil and reduce the appearance of pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Mix the harvested leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes then let it cool for 1 hour.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the liquid and heat gently until melted.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores, which can contribute to oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin, while also helping to regulate sebum production.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and accelerate aging, which can lead to oily skin; they also help to stabilize skin cell membranes, improving skin elasticity and texture.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with oily skin because of its unique astringent and balancing properties. The antiseptic and antifungal properties in lavender help to reduce inflammation and purify the pores, minimizing oil production. Additionally, its dehydrating effect helps to regulate sebum production, leaving the skin feeling smoother and looking more even-toned. The calming and soothing properties of lavender also help to reduce stress, a common contributor to oily skin, making it an effective and natural solution for achieving balanced skin.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce sebum production by inhibiting the activity of sebaceous glands, thereby controlling oil secretion and preventing clogged pores.
Linalyl acetate: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin, and promoting a balanced skin pH.
Caffeic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and bacteria growth on the skin, all of which contribute to oily skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants that help balance skin pH and reduce sebum production.
Flowers: Known for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help control oil production and prevent acne.
Stems: Contain flavonoids that help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 50g of distilled water for infusion.
Infuse the flowers in the distilled water for 10 minutes under medium heat.
Strain the mixture to obtain 20g of lavandula angustifolia infusion and discard the solids.
Combine 100g of shea butter and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Emulsify the mixture by slowly adding 50g of cooled lavandula angustifolia infusion to 50g of sweet almond oil.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, while also calming and soothing the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal aloe cream's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause damage to the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and protecting the skin from further environmental stressors.

Natural antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's natural antibacterial properties help to prevent bacterial growth and infection, which is especially important for oily skin prone to clogged pores and acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that balance and regulate the skin's natural oil production. The gel inside aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness. Additionally, aloe vera has natural astringent properties that help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oily skin and preventing clogged pores. This makes aloe vera creams an effective natural remedy for oily skin. Regular use can lead to a more balanced skin tone and reduced oil production.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin is a natural anthraquinone glycoside that has astringent properties, which help to reduce the production of sebum and minimize oiliness on the skin.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a lignan derivative that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to control acne and prevent clogged pores that can lead to oily skin.
Saponins: Saponins are a type of triterpene that have surfactant properties, which help to emulsify oils and reduce their viscosity, thereby minimizing the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in aloe-emodin, which helps to control oil production and reduce acne.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside the leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm oily skin.
Juice from Leaves: The juice from the leaves has been found to reduce sebum production, making it effective in controlling oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50 grams of aloe vera gel and 20 grams of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Melt the beeswax in low heat over 10 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Add 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter to the melted beeswax mixture.
Stir in 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of essential oil over 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and refrigerate for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas like green tea, chamomile, and lavender contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the skin, which can contribute to oiliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Teas like peppermint, ginger, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal teas like tea tree, chamomile, and calendula contain antibacterial agents that can help control acne-causing bacteria and other microorganisms on the skin, reducing oil production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that balance skin oil production. Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, these creams help to regulate the skin's natural oil barrier, reducing excess sebum production and minimizing pores. The anti-inflammatory properties of Camellia sinensis also soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation, resulting in a smoother, more even complexion that appears less oily and more radiant. Regular use can lead to a balanced and healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help regulate sebum production, reducing the oiliness of the skin.

Theaflavins: These flavonoids have been shown to inhibit the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to oily skin.
Gallic acid: This phenolic acid has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce the appearance of pores and control excess oil production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in tannins and catechins, which help to reduce oil production and balance the skin's natural pH, making them suitable for creams for oily skin.
Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm oily skin, reducing redness and irritation.
Leaves: They also have astringent properties that help to tighten pores, reducing oiliness and giving the skin a smoother appearance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of camellia sinensis leaves by hand to ensure maximum quality and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer for 2-3 hours at 150°f to remove moisture.
Steep 1 cup of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 2 cups of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the camellia sinensis infusion through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove solids immediately.
Mix 1/2 cup of the camellia sinensis infusion with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1/4 cup of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Ginger's antibacterial compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can clog pores and contribute to acne and oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and paradol, help to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can exacerbate conditions like acne, rosacea, and oily skin by increasing redness and swelling.
Astringent properties: Ginger's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, leaving skin looking cleaner and more balanced.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The active compounds present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to balance the skin's pH levels and reduce oil production. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. This results in a clearer, more balanced complexion and a reduction in the appearance of pores.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to reduce oil production and minimize pores on oily skin.
Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have antibacterial and antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of acne-causing bacteria and reducing inflammation associated with oily skin.

Shogaol: This ginger-derived compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin, reduce oil production, and prevent oxidative damage associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and soothe oily skin.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the skin's pH and reduce oil production.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to clean and calm oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from a trusted supplier or local market.
Grate the rhizomes into a fine paste using a microplane grater or sharp knife for 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of distilled water and 20 grams of glycerin in a saucepan and heat for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler and melt for 10 minutes.
Combine the grated rhizome paste, wax mixture, and water mixture in a blending bowl and blend for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compound, calendic acid, helps reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective treatment for reducing inflammation in oily skin.

Antibacterial property: The cream's antibacterial properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), help combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to clogged pores and excess oil production in oily skin.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and lead to signs of aging, such as fine lines and wrinkles, making it an effective treatment for mature or damaged skin prone to oiliness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with oily skin because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and irritation often associated with oily skin. Calendula's ability to balance the skin's pH level and promote a healthy skin environment helps to regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and preventing clogged skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly.
Mix 30g of dried calendula flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Whip the oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can lead to excessive oil production in the skin.

Sebum-regulating activity: Saw palmetto's beta-sitosterol content has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can stimulate sebaceous glands and lead to oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne and oily skin, allowing for clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, creams helps with oily skin because of its unique ability to regulate and balance sebum production. The extract from the saw palmetto plant has natural anti-androgen properties, which help to reduce the production of excess oil in the skin. This leads to a reduction in the appearance of large pores, a decrease in the severity of acne breakouts, and an overall improvement in skin texture and tone, leaving skin looking smoother and more balanced.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin and potentially preventing acne.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their astringent properties that help to reduce oil production and balance skin pH.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their ability to control sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them suitable for oily skin conditions.
Roots: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to regulate oil production and soothe irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried serenoa repens root powder, 200 grams of beeswax, 200 grams of coconut oil, and 100 grams of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Combine the melted mixture with the dried serenoa repens root powder and stir for 10 minutes to create a uniform blend.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and continue heating until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from the heat and pour it into a mold to cool and solidify for 60 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, which can exacerbate oily skin, promoting a calm and balanced complexion.

Additionally, chamomile's astringent properties help to regulate the skin's natural oil production, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, thereby controlling oil production and preventing clogged pores.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Matricaria chamomilla flowers are used to make creams for oily skin because they contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm and balance the skin.
Leaves: Matricaria chamomilla leaves are used to make creams for oily skin because they possess astringent properties that help reduce oil production and purify the skin.
Seeds: Matricaria chamomilla seeds are used to make creams for oily skin because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce acne and soothe the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reliable source and store them in an airtight container.
Mix 10 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 20 grams of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at a temperature of 60-70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, thereby helping to control oil production and reduce sebum buildup.

Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. This natural remedy also helps to balance the skin's pH, promoting a healthy and balanced complexion. As a result, rosemary creams can be an effective and natural solution for managing oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid helps with oily skin by its ability to inhibit the production of sebum, thereby reducing the appearance of oiliness.
Carnosic Acid: A phenolic diterpene, carnosic acid helps with oily skin by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and prevent further sebum production.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for about 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the solid rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Weigh out 10% of the infused oil's weight in beeswax and melt it in a double boiler with a gentle heat.
Gradually mix the melted beeswax with the infused oil in a 10 to 1 ratio by weight and stir well to combine.
Pour the hot herbal oil mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antibacterial compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphor, help reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, preventing clogged pores and breakouts associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum, like globoid and eugenol, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation that can exacerbate oily skin conditions.

Astringent properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's astringent compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, controlling oiliness and leaving the skin feeling clean and refreshed.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of acne and other oily skin-related issues. remedy_recipe: Combine 500ml of distilled water with 100g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Add 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to the infused water in a heatproof bowl.
Stir the mixture over low heat until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully dissolved and the mixture cools to around 40 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The menthol in peppermint cools the skin, reducing inflammation and excess oil production, leaving pores unclogged and refined. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to calm redness and irritation associated with acne-prone skin. remedy_properties: Cooling and Astringent Properties: Peppermint's cooling effect helps to reduce inflammation and constrict pores, while its astringent properties help to dry out excess oil and tighten the skin, making it an effective treatment for reducing oiliness.

Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to oily skin by reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Toning and Balancing Properties: Peppermint's toning effect helps to balance the skin's natural pH, reduce sebum production, and regulate oil flow, resulting in a more even-toned and balanced complexion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Blend 50 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a steeping mixture.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes allowing the menthol and flavonoids to infuse into the water.
Combine the cooled infusion with 200 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax, gently heating the mixture until the wax is fully melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with oily skin because they contain natural astringent properties that help to balance and regulate sebum production. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground pelargonium graveolens with beeswax and sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3:6 by weight and heat until melted in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add jojoba oil and glycerin to the mixture at a ratio of 2:1 by volume and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a reputable source.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the filtered oil.
Mix one part of the filtered thymus vulgaris oil with three parts of beeswax and heat until melted then pour into sterile cream jars cooling to form a creamy consistency.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in yarrow cream, such as camphor and borneol, have antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to control acne-causing bacteria, reducing breakouts and preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of saponins, which help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce excess oil production.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain acacetin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe and calm oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh acmilea millefolium leaves and stems, clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine one part dried achillea millefolium herb with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f for 4-6 hours to extract beneficial properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and measuring final volume.
Mix 10% of the herbal infusion with beeswax and vitamin e oil, melt at 120°f for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that work to balance the skin's natural oil production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of lemongrass help to reduce inflammation and purify the pores, which in turn reduces excess sebum secretion. Additionally, lemongrass has astringent properties that help to tighten the pores, making it an effective remedy for controlling oily skin. By using lemongrass creams, individuals with oily skin can achieve a more balanced and healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin's surface, which can contribute to acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: The astringent and antiseptic properties of lemongrass oil help to tighten pores and reduce inflammation, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving skin looking smoother and more refined.

Antifungal and decongestant properties: Lemongrass oil's antifungal properties can help to combat fungal infections that can cause skin issues like acne and redness, while its decongestant properties can help to reduce puffiness and tighten the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to balance oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin, making them effective in treating acne-prone skin.
Stems: The stems of lemongrass contain a high concentration of antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that help to purify the skin and control sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and dry them in the shade for four hours to ensure optimal potency.
Grind dried lemongrass into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and mix with 20g of coconut oil.
Combine the lemongrass powder mixture with 10ml of distilled water and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Add 5g of beeswax and 2g of shea butter to the paste and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes to melt the waxes.
Pour the melted cream into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for three hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with oily skin because they are rich in salicin, a beta hydroxy acid that deep cleans pores and reduces excess oil production. The cream's natural extracts also help to balance the skin's pH, controlling sebum production and preventing clogged pores. Additionally, maidenhair tree contains antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily skin. As a result, using maidenhair tree creams can leave your skin looking smoother, clearer, and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal maidenhair tree creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress on the skin, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions.

Soothing and calming properties: The creams' ability to soothe and calm the skin can help reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin, allowing it to regulate its natural oil production and appear more balanced.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal maidenhair tree creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can cause pores to enlarge and lead to excess oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to control sebum production and reduce inflammation in oily skin.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin while reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass container until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of jojoba oil, 10 drops of tea tree oil, and 5 grams of kaolin clay to the mixture stirring thoroughly for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture at low temperature (30°c) for 1 hour while stirring occasionally to facilitate infusion of the herbal extract.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with oily skin because of its unique ability to balance the skin's natural oil production. The herbal extract contained in these creams, Echinacea pallida, has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores. Additionally, Kansas coneflower cream's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce their appearance, leaving the skin looking smooth and refined. This natural formula makes it an effective treatment for oily skin, reducing shine and excess oil while maintaining healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and preventing damage to the skin caused by excess oil production.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Kansas coneflower cream reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, helping to soothe and calm the skin without drying it out.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents in Kansas coneflower cream help control bacteria that can contribute to acne and oily skin, preventing breakouts and reducing the appearance of pores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help regulate sebum production and reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
Fruits: Containing a high concentration of fatty acids, fruits provide moisturizing properties that soothe and calm the skin without clogging pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers at 20 grams and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground echinacea powder with sweet almond oil at 30 milliliters and beeswax at 5 grams in a double boiler setup for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture under low heat until the beeswax is fully melted, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes total preparation time.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 70°c before whipping with an electric whisk for 10 minutes to emulsify the ingredients.
Pour the whipped cream into a sterilized glass jar, allowing it to cool completely at room temperature or in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to balance sebum production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties in sage cream help to reduce inflammation and combat acne-causing bacteria, making it an effective treatment for clogged pores and excess oil buildup. Additionally, the astringent properties of sage help to tighten pores, reducing their appearance and minimizing the shine associated with oily skin. This results in a more refined, healthier-looking complexion that is less prone to oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions like acne.
Astringent properties: The tannins in sage have an astringent effect, helping to reduce sebum production and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling oiliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, sage rhyzomes have natural astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and balance oily skin.
Barks: Sage barks contain tannins and phenolic acids that can help to tighten pores, reduce redness, and prevent bacterial growth on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of salvia officinalis, ensure cleanliness and air-dry them for 2 weeks.
Combine dried salvia leaves with sweet almond oil in a 10-15% ratio by weight for 4 hours.
Infuse the mixture at room temperature, shaking occasionally, for 6 days and then strain it.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 30-40% ratio by weight for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture gently under low heat until beeswax is fully melted and emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to balance oil production and reduce sebum secretion. The herbal extract contains compounds that target the sebaceous glands, regulating their activity and preventing excess oil from accumulating on the surface of the skin. This results in a smoother, more even-toned complexion that appears less greasy and shiny, while also reducing the appearance of pores. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective natural solution for managing oily skin concerns.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Fennel's antibacterial compounds, such as anethole, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin that can contribute to clogged pores and acne.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin and acne.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the skin and contribute to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing sebum production and soothing acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Known for their natural astringent and antiseptic properties, helping to balance oil production and prevent clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground fennel powder with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for 15 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 10 drops of lavender essential oil into the fennel-coconut oil mixture until the wax is fully dissolved for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 120°f before whipping it with an electric mixer or whisk for 2 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars or tubes, seal them tightly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until opened.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oily skin are topical treatments infused with natural herbs that help regulate oil production and reduce acne, providing a balanced complexion. The best herbal creams for oily skin include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Saponaria officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Geranium maculatum, Zingiber officinale, Cinchona officinalis, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, oily skin can lead to acne, social anxiety, and low self-esteem, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal creams are acne, rosacea, and eczema. These conditions often exhibit symptoms such as inflammation, redness, and irritation, which herbal creams can help soothe and calm. Herbal ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile are commonly used in these creams to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with oily skin is a blend of Tea Tree Oil and Aloe Vera creams. Tea Tree Oil has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to control acne and reduce oil production. Aloe Vera, on the other hand, soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and inflammation. When combined, these creams provide a balanced and non-greasy solution to manage oily skin, promoting a healthier and more even complexion. This duo is a natural and effective way to control oiliness and prevent breakouts.
